info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Appointment Management API
info.description: The Appointment Management API allows businesses to efficiently schedule and manage appointments within their service offerings. This API provides functionality for creating new appointments, enabling service providers to streamline customer interactions and optimize service delivery. Typical consumers include service-based organizations such as clinics, salons, and consulting firms, who require a reliable system for appointment scheduling and management.

post.description: This endpoint allows users to create a new appointment by providing the necessary details. Ensure that all required fields are filled for successful appointment creation.

requestBody.description: This request body is used to create a new appointment. It must contain all necessary details for the appointment, following the defined schema.

500.description: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

400.description: The request contains invalid parameters or data.

200.description: The request was successful and the response contains appointment data.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: ErrorResponse

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the error occurred.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with the error.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

message.description: A detailed message explaining the error.
message.example: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

path.description: The request path that caused the error.

trace.description: A stack trace or additional debugging information.

suggestion.example: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Schema representing the error response for a failed appointment operation.

example.message: Unable to process appointment request due to internal service failure.

details.message: Database connection timeout while saving appointment details.

example.suggestion: Please retry after some time or contact support if the issue persists.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.title: ErrorDetails

service.description: The name of the service that encountered the error.

errorCode.description: A specific error code related to the error.

message.description: A message providing additional context about the error.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Error.description: Schema representing detailed information about the error.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Error Response for Appointment Operations

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the error occurred.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with the error.

error.description: A short description of the error type.

message.description: A detailed message describing the error.

path.description: The path of the request that generated the error.

trace.description: A trace of the error for debugging purposes.

validationErrors.description: An array of validation error details for the request fields.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for correcting the error.
suggestion.example: Please provide valid patient and doctor appointment details.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Represents the structure of an error response returned by the appointment operations when a request fails due to client-side validation errors.

example.suggestion: Please provide valid patient and doctor appointment details.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Valid.title: Validation Error Details

field.description: The name of the field that failed validation.

rejectedValue.description: The value that was rejected due to validation failure.

message.description: A message explaining why the value was rejected.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Valid.description: Represents a validation error for a specific field in the request.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.title: Appointment Creation Request

patientId.description: The unique identifier of the patient.

doctorId.description: The unique identifier of the doctor.

appointmentDate.description: The date of the appointment in ISO 8601 format.

appointmentTime.description: The time of the appointment in HH:mm format.

department.description: The name of the department where the appointment will take place.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: This schema represents the request body for creating a new appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Insur.title: Insurance Details

provider.description: The name of the insurance provider.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Insur.description: This schema contains insurance information for the patient.

appointmentId.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

patientId.description: The unique identifier for the patient associated with the appointment.

doctorId.description: The unique identifier for the doctor assigned to the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., confirmed, cancelled, completed).

appointmentDate.description: The date of the appointment in YYYY-MM-DD format.

appointmentTime.description: The time of the appointment in HH:MM format.

department.description: The department where the appointment is scheduled (e.g., Cardiology, Orthopedics).

message.description: An optional message related to the appointment.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createAppointment-Creat.description: Response object containing appointment details after successfully creating an appointment.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates which request headers can be varied in cache responses. This helps in content negotiation based on client request headers.

Location.description: Specifies the URI of the newly created resource after a successful POST request. This is typically used for redirection.

content-type.description: Indicates the media type of the resource returned in the response. This informs the client about the format of the content.
